parsed.final_response: It sounds like you're caught between wanting to see your dad become more independent and fearing that he'll slip up and you'll have to pick up the slack again. That fear is really understandable, given how much responsibility you've taken on as his caregiver. Can you tell me more about what it would mean for you to see him come back on time and ask to walk alone - what would that say about your ability to trust him and yourself?

The strategies that might be helpful in this moment are support and complex reflection, as they can help you process your emotions and gain a deeper understanding of your thoughts and feelings. The evidence that stands out to me is your consistent expression of fear and guilt about not being able to care for your dad perfectly, and the weight of responsibility you've placed on yourself. My entry point for this response will be to acknowledge the fear and guilt you're feeling, and to help you explore what it would mean to trust your dad to handle things on his own.

parsed.final_response: You're really scared that if your dad goes on a walk alone, he might slip up and you'll have to pick up the pieces, and that would feel like a failure to you. It's like you're always waiting for the other shoe to drop, and that's a really tough way to live. Can you imagine what it would be like to trust your dad to handle things on his own, even if it's just for a short time, and to see that as a success rather than a failure?
